text: T mobile/ Sprint I wouldn't sign a contract with them very easy to sign up but when it comes time to cancel it's like arassment 35 + minutes on the phone.had to talk to 4 people they keep asking why are you canceling.I called for a simple cancel it turn into be a problem they need a all new Customer service team it should be very easy to sign up and to cancel . Not 4 people 35+ minutes later Customer service thumbs down

I came to T-Mobile because they offered better price for the offer. However there over the phone customer service was horrible. I was kept on hold for over 1 hour and 45 minutes. He pretended to help me while he took breaks with me waiting on hold. If you decide to use them just know your time ñor mental state is not a concern.

I have been with them for 10 years, they will not match AT&T S22 trade in deal even when i told them i was cancelling account because of it. Also girlfriend has AT&T already and she always gets better service in rural areas. AT&T same price with better service and better trade in deals.

I contacted TMobile on several occasions to request they mail bills to my Mom for her cell phone account without any success. They could not explain why they stopped mailing bills to her because she NEVER requested paperless statements!!!!! I was given the run around so many times I made decision to switch her cell phone to my cell phone carrier.  The worse customer no service I ever experienced!!!!
I physically went to a T-Mobile store and spoke to the most awesome, professional customer service rep named Yvette (Brass Mill Mall-Waterbury, Ct.) She listened to my story and immediately took action to assist me.  Yvette went above and beyond the call of duty.  She was able to honor my request to have bills mailed to my Mom after spending several minutes on the phone with her fellow TMobile colleague and advising him how important it was to service and satisfy the customer. I'm so happy she was able to resolve this situation and illustrate the true meaning of a customer advocate.  Her patience and determination to make every effort to assist me was truly awesome. Yvette 's superb skills and efforts to resolve this very simple request are the only reason I decided not to switch to another carrier.   Thank you Yvette!!!!!!

I recently became a t mobile customer.  I made the decision to switch from Verizon because of the great offer they gave me.  As the months went buy  I asked about the $200 that was suppose to be credited to my account.  So they told me that they divide it up and credit my account on a monthly basis, and it takes a month or so to hit my account. So I finally realized that something wasn’t right.  Instead of getting the $200 I was promised I only got $100.  Apparently what happened, I was given the wrong amount by someone who no longer works there.   I was disappointed but Emanuel was awesome.  He took to the phone to call customers service to get things straightened out.  It took 30 minutes or so.  The first time calling didn’t get anywhere but he was determined to fix my situation. Called back and asked for a supervisor and finally got through.  He explained the situation again. This time they finally worked with him and was able to get my a credit for the difference. I was very appreciative and he wasn’t about to give up.   I am an Assistant Operations Manager for Rite Aid. I know what great service is.    This young man has great customer service skills. If you are looking to switch or get a new service he is up on the deals and is willing to go above and beyond.  Go see Emanuel at T Mobile in the Loop in Methuen   You won’t be disappointed.

On 2-15-22 and 2-16-22 I went to the T-Mobile store #339D, 1481 NJ-23 Unit STE 7, Butler, NJ 07405.  I met 3 employees: Ahnaf, Rami and Brenda.  I was completely overwhelmed  by the outstanding customer service by Ahnaf, Rami and Brenda.  They were very friendly, courteous and their knowledge was at the highest level.  They took care of all my telephone needs and service!  Thank you!

TMobile home internet is great, however signed up on 12/25/2021, received two units on 12/28/21. Tried for over a month to get a hold of someone on the phone to correct the issue of two units only to find out we had to drive an hour to the tmobile store. Customer service in the Elizabeth City NC store(Tanglewood Dr) was excellent. Representative Nik got us a new pin# for the unit we have hooked up and cancelled the account on the second one. We were to receive a return label  by email. That did not happen. Called customer service and they said sorry we can't help you. That account has been cancelled. Called Nik at the store we had previously visited, told him what was going on. He said let me call you back. Within 15 minutes, Nik called me back and told me to check my email. Sure enough, there was the label. Nik gave us the best customer service that I have had with a company in a very long time. The 5 star rating is for the service provided by Nik at the store.

On 01/23/2022 I made a call to T-Mobile Customer Service Department, with the intention of requesting information about their Wi-Fi internet service. The Representative was very kind and offered me a 1-month free trial for this service, she explained to me that they were going to send me a hot spot and that if I didn't like the service, I could return the device in the closest T-Mobile store.
I got the device 2 days later and I started using it, unfortunately the WIFI service didn't meet my expectation, the internet connection was very unstable and didn't meet my needs. So, I decided that I was going to visit the closest T-Mobile store to return the hot spot.
On Sunday 02/13/2022 when I reviewed my account I noticed that the charge for this service was already included in my invoice, o I called Customer Service to explain the situation and let them know that I didn't want the service and request the fix of the invoice, that call was a complete mess, more than 40 minutes on the phone, I talked with 4 different representatives and none of them was able to solve my situation, instead they out me on hold. I ended the call and decided to just go personally to the closest T-Mobile store and solve the problem in person.
Today 02/15/2022 I visited the closest T-Mobile store and the representative there told me that they cannot receive the hot spot, made me call the Customer Service line again and they told me that to return the hot spot is a very difficult process because it has been already more than 14 days! This is unacceptable! I feel that they lied to me just to sell me a service, and I really don't like this, I've been a T-Mobile Customer for more than 7 year now and this is not the way to treat loyal customers! I'll keep insisting to be able to solve my issue, but I'm feeling very frustrated at this point!

If you do not stream live TV then this will probably be fine. However, if you do stream live TV this is a nightmare service. The IP address of your tower changes every few weeks and then shuts down your live streaming service because it thinks you are not home with the new IP address. DO NOT USE THIS FOR STREAMING LIVE TV!!!!!

TMobile - Where do I begin, at this point I would tell you to run. Run fast and far. I am a Customer of several years. T-Mobile sold me two phones under a "promotion" and turns out that particular model of Motorola phone will not work on their 5G network. So, I have two lines with non-working phones (one belongs to a child and the other a HEALTH CARE WORKER! just purchased in December 2021. To make matters worse both managers at local stores I visited (SAM in particular on Carlisle) have completely disregarded the issue and not even bothered to call back after multiple visits/calls. The District Manager is unavailable and all I can get is a mortified agent apologizing for the lack of any resolution over the last THREE WEEKS! AGAIN, NON-WORKING PHONES T-Mobile sold and will not replace! I am a single mom struggling to keep up and to have been forced to spend over 7 1/2 hours already dealing with this issue is unacceptable. NO RESOLUTION...NO CONCERN FROM MANAGEMENT.I feel stupid and taken advantage of for trusting this company to honor what they say they will do. DO NOT TRUST ANYTHING. ASK QUESTIONS, THEN ASK AGAIN TO SEE IF YOU GET THE SAME ANSWER...GOOD LUCK IF YOU EVER NEED ANY ASSISTANCE WITH A NON-STANDARD PHONE ISSUE!
You are not a customer that is valued…
T-Mobile is fantastic! Thanks for saving me $150 monthly by taking me away from both Verizon and Hargray! Very happy with customer service as well. All the way around they are the better choice!

Had service for three day sent phone back    
Now they trying to change me for a month after I canceled it, now they sent it to Collections worst company to deal with. After I was told it was taken care of by three different people

T-Mobile and Sprint!!!!! When T-Mobile took over Sprint , they could not get my phone(NOTE 20 PLUS ULTRA) to work on their new system. So, after a month of being without a phone and not getting it resolved, I decided to go with another carrier. I sent the phone back, and my bill was paid. Now, they want to send me collections  for the remaining cost of the lease on the phone that they got back. They have proof that they got the phone back. They said I was still under the lease on it even though they could not provide service to it. I've been going through this with them for a year now. I have called and been on hold numerous times for over an hour, trying to talk to a supervisor, and no one will help. I have been promised that one will call me... to no avail! This is now going to affect my credit.  I had been with Sprint for over 10 years...
T-Mobile has the worst customer service and phone service, and the local stores can't help. Jeers T-Mobile

This is the worst company I’ve have ever done business with as far as customer service. I have called at least 10 times in last 4mo this and every time I call they send you to foreign country with someone reading a checklist that they apparently they don't know how to help for the issue you are calling but quick to upswell while your frustrated just dumb practice! After an hour or so of no help they ship next level which is another 30 mins and you got a 50-50 chance they will actually help fix problem till you ask for supervisor then Its oh how sorry they are and maybe your issue is addressed!?!?!  if I ran my small company that way I would be out  of business!! It’s so bad I will leaving with all my company’s phone services  to another carrier soon!!! Absolutely  not the best of Puget sounds local companies embarrassment!!!! 

Jeff Fieck customer for 13 years

The worst customer service ever!!  Poorly trained employees.  Once my contract is up, will be switching to another mobile carrier.  Wiped all contacts off of my telephone and did not know how to retrieve them.

lucky there isnt 0 stars because thats what theyd have gotten they told me there new internet service would work in my area  i traded my phone for a new one  received everything was happy until after 3hrs i find out it wont work in my area  i bought screen savers and an otterbox to protect my purchase and now im out of luck  im trying to get my internet back but cant get the same pan  T-MOBILE I WANT YOU TO KNOW YOU ARE THE HORRIBLE COMPANY OUT THERE  LIARS AND A THIEFS  PEOPLE STAY AWAY FROM T-MOBILE

I had a good experience at Surfside T-Mobil branch today. Dominicea was so kind as I enter the store and Mykel was very helpful taking the time to respond my questions. Thank you for the courtesy to both of you. Keep up the kindness, good manners and work ethics.

edits.text: I love to go personally to pay my bill at Surfside branch and see smiling faces on happy employees willing to give their best service to clients. No matter how busy they are, they always find a little time to make you feel comfortable while waiting. Last time, Daniela and Antonio were giving me their best assistance while representing high T mobile company. Thanks to all of you and specially to the ones who know how to chose the best employees!

text:
OMG!!! I don't even know where to start!!! My problem goes way back, but I'll tell this one. I had noticed that I was still being charged for a phone I had purchased for a second line. I ended up giving it to my niece. After a year or so the phone got broken. I called them to cancel the phone and the insurance. They assured me that they did that. Well come to find out they did no such thing. Again I called them AND went in!!! They assured me that it was all taken care of.  
Long story short, I call back to follow up on our conversation where agent assured me that he was going to take care of my dilemma. Constantly apologizing as the all do! He gets on to apologize again and tell me there is nothing he can do for me. After more pleading I ask to speak to a manager/supervisor. He was hesitant. Finally he gets somebody. A woman came on and "apologizes" and says there is nothing she can do. They both say that in the notes, there is no mention of canceling the second line and insurance. I said I most certainly did. It's the incompetence of the agent I spoke to, that did not take and record proper notes. I said, in the beginning of every call is a recording that says "this call will be recorded for quality purposes". I said GO BACK and listen to your recordings. This woman proceeded to argue with me. She hesitated to give me her name and Badge #. She said it would do me no good. She ended up giving it to me. 
I was told that I cannot cancel this over the phone which was never previously told to me. They said I needed to send an email requesting the cancellation. 
I went on to the app under my account and OMG!!! I am and have been being charged tons of money for features, insurance and what not. Let me say that when I called to cancel the 2nd line and insurance, I said make sure that I still have insurance on the primary line. It's the one I use and very important to me. "Oh yes ma'am" "that will stay the same". Well guess what??? I find out that I have not had insurance on my primary line for all of this time. It's a good darn thing that I didn't need it!!!  I am a senior who lives on a very low fixed income. I called in to see if I could somehow rearrange my plan to lower it because I am on the verge of being homeless. (Basically I am at the moment) sleeping on couches of friends. That is how I again realized T MOBILE is stealing money from me. By this time she is blaring at me and not letting me speak. I of course trying to get in a word or two I ask to speak to her manager/supervisor. She said she didn't have one. I said EVERYBODY has a boss or supervisor. Especially in a large company like T MOBILE. She said, well I don't, I'm the boss"!!! I find that hard to believe considering the conversation we were having!!! 
I am so upset. They have been charging me so much money. I am totally broke and don't get another SS check till the 3rd of March. I don't know what to do. I'm feeling so depressed and desperate.  
Thank you in advance for any suggestions on how to handle this.

Maybe the worst customer service to ever exist. I signed up as a new customer Friday. Phone wouldn’t arrive until Tuesday. I was told multiple times with tax, phone (on promotion), and monthly service it would be $96. The same price I paid with Verizon. I never received tracking for my phone so I called to check on it. My bill is 180!! *not 96 dollars*….My name is spelled wrong and that is apparently not fixable….and I’m 6 calls in trying to resolve this. For a major company it feels like a new, small, unorganized company. All the “experts” aka agents do is apologize but 0 action. 

Can’t wait to get out of my contract and stick with Verizon. Terrible customer service. Disorganized. Business model feels dirty and like a major scam.

We switched over to TMobile internet in Nov 2021 and it has been an absolute nightmare. The first few weeks it seemed pretty good but ever since about Dec 15 we wake up EVERY morning with no internet or internet speeds that remind me of the old dialup days. It remains like this for most of the day and then we will get a few hours in the evening where it seems to be good. This has been going on for 2 months now and every time we call support we get no answers. They always want to run you thru the standard procedures which never work and then they just say oh they are working on upgrading the system. I now call “bs”. We don’t know what to do. Calling their support numbers is totally useless.

I went on a cruise last November and I was overcharged $605 even though I called to make sure that we could use the internet. 

1. On 11/28/21 (before the ship sailed), I called T-mobile to see if they had any packages for cruises and international calling for Mexico and the Bahamas. The customer service representative said yes, it will be $35 for 3 lines for 10 days, and it needed to be paid instantly so we paid $105 total.

2. When the ship was at sea, we had no signal or internet. When we landed in the Bahamas, we called T-mobile and they told us that they had made a mistake and that T-mobile doesn't directly deal with Royal Caribbean: The Oddysey. She offered to take off $50 for their mistake and said that we could still use the internet when the boat landed. Because of that, we had to pay an extra $26 extra for each day on the cruise. 

3. When we came home, we got a bill from T-mobile for a $605 service charge because we didn't turn our phones on airplane mode when the boat was at sea. We already paid extra for the cruise and paid extra for T-mobile for internet that wasn't even available. The customer service representative didn't tell us to turn on airplane mode on our phones while we were on land so we didn't know it could incur charges. 

4. We have called T-mobile every week since we got back and every time, customer service lets us know that they will give the $605 credit back to us and we should receive the credit card refund within a week but each time we wait, nothing happens. 

5. Last week, we had contact with a supervisor named Jim. He said that he would not do anything about that because it is "our problem" and that they could only credit us back for a total of $105. He also said, "Go to court, I don't care." We also got a note today that we have to take care of the bill or they will charge us $20 a day for each line by tomorrow 2/8/22.

6. My family and I have been with T-mobile for over 10 years and we travel often. In order to not be charged extra for the bill, we always buy the international package but we did not expect to be turned away like this when we asked for T-mobile's help even though we have been loyal to them for over a decade. We are reconsidering switching over to another company because of the way we have been treated.

I started with T-mobile service around 5 months ago. The moment I stated the service I had issues with the connections and data and texting service. I called many times and they keep telling me that they are working on getting the service Better in my area which I was not told this before I participated in their service. Today they told me that my phone is not compatible with T-mobile and they wanted me to replace my I-phone with I know brand. Really bad:(

Compared to Verizon and AT&T, T-Mobile has been a breath of fresh air. Their support people seem to be located in the United States, they actually know how to fix and deal with issues, and they're friendly.  T-Mobile's 5GUC service is blazing fast and free of issues and problems.  The billing and online portal from T-Mobile absolutely blows away Verizon's portal; T-Mobile's portal is simple, intuitive and concise, where Verizon's is a bloated mess that is always broken.

Service was excellent until they screwed me out of my trade in money. Was promised $300 trade in. They retroactively removed the trade in and never notified me. I just noticed the higher bill. Then they told me it was too late to do anything about it and kept my phone and money. Then two different CS told me they'd change my plan to a lower bill and didn't. Last straw... Vax mandates and firing employees. I'm out.
They will keep charging your credit card after you leave
Ive been a customer for over three years . The whole time it has been a nightmare! My bill is constantly being changed. My "upgrades " have cost me thousands and management and staff are rude .

Big shout out to T Mobiles Facebook agents. I left Verizon for enticement of cheaper more straight forward plans and coverage where I needed it with 5G. Well while prices were straight forward promised coverage was less then friendly and not there at all. I use my phone for work and personal including school and hotspot so it's not optional not to have service where needed. I reached out through the app via chat and to the local store and got the run around. The Facebook team resolved my issue which ended in a no charge to me end of my service enabling me to go back to my previous carrier. The Facebook team resolved my issue and made sure I was satisfied and are still checking in. The rest of the team should take lessons from them.

I joined T-Mobile after leaving Verizon in September of 2021. I went on a cruise in December and I called T-Mobile and asked if I could use the WiFi calling feature to make calls while I was on board the ship and the T-Mobile expert told me yes. So I purchased the WiFi plan for four people and a month later I received a bill for 2415 a difference of 2305 my regular bill was 110 a month for my family. At first I was impressed with T-Mobile and the reps but the more I spoke with them I seen that it was just fluff and nothing else. I would hear, T-Mobile got your back and your the best part of TMobile. I eventually ended up getting a supervisor and told them what happened and I asked if they could listen to the call and they told me yes so eventually they told me that because they listened to the call they would take off half which it was only 1000 dollars that they took off. This company doesn't stand behind what they say, they are strictly out for more customers by trying their fluff. Don't believe it. You are not the best part of TMobile you are the worst part of their competition and they want you so they can't have you. It's simple do what you say and mean what you what you say and you would get better results. Beware. T-Mobile has deceptive business practices and overall the service sucks. I am now with Visible powered by Verizon and they have party pay ( smiling loudly22) I have yet talked to a person but we will see what happens.

T-Mobile expert Darius, Ashburn, VA was very helpful, efficient, knowledgeable and professional in helping me out with phone connectivity issues. This was after being turned away from the T-Mobile Leesburg, VA store and told that I had to contact T-Mobile via their help line. Stay away from T-Mobile Leesburg store. Driving 12 minutes further is worth the service.

So unfortunate that T-mobile does not stand behind there insurance plan.  My son dropped his phone, we called T-Mobile they said they would send us a replacement. We received the replacement and sent back the damaged phone in the box provided thinking it would be processed. Only to have received a phone that the Sim card was bad. We went to the store and had to PAY for a new Sim card even though we just got the phone. My son had the phone for less than a week, went back to T-Mobile and again T-Moble did not honor the defective phone, the manager at the store said it wasn't on them. So we took our T-Mobile defective phone to Apple and they confirmed the phone  was defective. We received a new  phone fromApple AND T-Mobile charges us $300.00 telling us the phone that was damaged was not covered under insurance . We have called T-Mobile 4 times to try and understand why we pay monthly for insurance only to be told we can't use it. SO, I will be canceling my account worth T-Mobile and I will plaster my complaint all over my social media platforms, friends, family Google, yelp and any another review sites I can find letting everyone know to use VERIZON .

It has good reputation as cell phone carrier, however during my 20 years relation with them I noticed a lot of discripances .
As long as you don't question your bill, you are a good customer and would be treated as a king
Question the added fees and you get no reply, at the beginning, they will waive it for you , that would make you feel that you are in charge. But when repeated more times under different names, you will accept it
Finally, I could not coup with it as I got to old to play games, so I decided to move away to another co.
Guess what , 2 months later they came after me with a new bill, they called it expenses during the final stage of the account closing
They wouldn't let it go even 2 month after I closed the account.

As a longtime Sprint customer I went to a T-Mobile store to do an early transition based on a deal in which I got new discounted phones. I made it clear that I wanted to buy the phones outright at what I assumed would be the discount price and was told I could do so after the first month's bill. I then paid for the phones and lo and behold the second bill comes and I'm still being charged for the phones I assumed I had paid for. Now I entered the T-Mobile hall of mirrors, where each new person you talk to gives you a different story and spins you around with all sorts of convoluted information, until ultimately you discover that the information you received from the "closer" at the store was incorrect. You have now experienced T-Mobile's dysfunctional "the customer is always wrong" corporate culture and realize you've been had. The new, enlarged T-Mobile is certainly not an improved one, so steer clear. You'd probably do better dealing with the mob for a wireless plan than you will with these publicly traded thieves.

Should also be a option with 0 stars for T Mobile company, because I believe that one star it’s too much for them. I was lied to by their Ofer  that says buy one and get one for free if you’re adding a new line and I have to pay an extra 300$ for my second phone. I order a white device and they send me a blue one. The manager of the store that I was ordering my devices told me that he’s a. retailer and he’s not  responsible for the fake promotions and mistakes so think about ???  Looks more like a scam then a serious company.

Update:  hurray!!!  T-Mobile credited our mobile account $784.94!  

I posted a story on the company’s FB page with hashtag TMobile and their customer service page and my FB page.  This detailed the facts on our reward and how we did not receive what was promised on the Bring Your Own Device promotion this last Fall (which is a current program too). 

After waiting over the requisite month for the reward payment and reaching out to Tmobile multiple times I was repeatedly and firmly denied by customer service at all levels in all departments. 

Having had enough frustration with “customer service” I reached out to the BBB and the FCC and was at the point of contacting our State’s Attorney General. 

I might note too that I did get an immediate response from the Tmobile T-force team when I hashtagged TMobile on their Facebook page, my FB page and the Tmobile customer feedback page. After lengthy messaging (don’t give up on that thread of communication) the T-force folks promised to try another claim and offer a full credit as a last resort in 2 weeks (which was today). 

I highly encourage anyone who gets shut down by Tmobile when you file your claim for reward to not give up. Hit them from the social media side and/or the offical complaint side with BBB. 

I was thrilled to get what was promised and look forward to a better relationship with TMobile. 

Don’t give up!  And let me know if you need encouragement in your attempt.

Here’s the original story:
We have been customers with another cellular service for nearly 20 years.  There are 4 phone users in my family.  We made the bold move to “bring your phone” to #T-Mobile and paid off 2 of our remaining contracts and were promised we would receive up to $800 for the move to T-Mobile.  Before crossing over to the new network we sent in our screenshots to T-Mobile and filled out the paperwork.  We were advised we would need to wait 6-8 weeks for the credit from T-Mobile.  After waiting for 3 months we called customer service and were told our paperwork was never filed and that we were past the one month deadline to re file our paperwork to receive our promotional amount.  When we were transferred to the promotional dept they said the same and that customer service would “escalate” this case and contact us.  No contact.  We called customer service in another week or two and were now told that the 2 month deadline had elapsed 5 days before our follow up call and that nothing could be done since we missed the deadline.  All this to say that we filed everything the day T-Mobile got 4 new customers to sign up with them.  They did not send us any of what we were promised, denied receiving our paperwork, told us we only had one month to file the paperwork, then told us they had extended that deadline to 3 months, and then told us that we did not meet any of their deadlines even with repeated calls to their reward dept and their customer service department.  I spent over a 6 hours speaking with representatives at T-Mobile feeling like I had when I was with AT&T.  I had hoped we were not dealing with another corporate company who just shuffled us around, but I soon learned T-Mobile was treating us the same way: with little regard for us as long term customers paying monthly.  We did nothing wrong on our end.  Is this the way a phone service treats its newest LONG term customers?  I will tell everyone our story when they say that T-Mobile is making promises to get new customers!  This might help you consider too what phone company you would like to pay monthly for multiple years.

I Signed up January the 31st at Sprint/T-Mobile and they said I was qualified and it was completed I signed the contact and all then call me back saying I need to reapply something went wrong okay I did it again and then again and again no more I see how bad the customer service is I would not dare to be a customer I can only imagine how that would go with do not recommend them to anyone the called me every day and I had to call them all this week I told them they should of got all this taken care of in one day will never try again don’t waist time on this company

We moved our service from T-Mobile to a new provider on 1/13/2022. This was in the middle of our billing cycle and we were paid in full at the time. T-Mobile acknowledged the transfer to the new provider and the transfer was smooth. On 2/4/2022, T-Mobile debited my bank account, via debit card, for an additional month's service. I called T-Mobile and was told that they would process any money owed me in 30 days. They were completely unhelpful and condescending. T-Mobile owed me for approximately two weeks of service from the billing cycle I had paid for prior to the move. 
I had been a T-Mobile customer for many years. Their pricing simply got too expensive and their customer service deteriorated to an infuriating level. The customer service agents, both on the phone and in their stores, are unhelpful, condescending, and snarky. I will never do service with them again! Apparently, customer good will is not important to them.

Tmobile is a deceptive company. They have lied to us about our devices and plans. Then 4 months later they remove any offers we signed off on when we bought our phones and switched to the. They say there werr issues with the phones. But they can't provide anything showing or stating the issues, they can't give the phones back either. And these are phones that they inspected at the store and the store sent them in for us. How can  they state after having the phone in their possession for 4 months that now there is an issues. They are deceitful and use this tactic to get you to sign up and buy new phones then they charge you full price for them and you have no recourse. I've spent numerous hrs on the phone and they have not helped at all. They are not able to provide any information on the damage on the phone or evidence it happened prior to them having the phone for 4 months. on top of that we have horrible cell service, our brand new phones lock up all the time, we don't get or recieve text messages for days at a time, and calls don't even come through. I would recommend stealing clear of this T mobile and find a more trust worthy brand even if you have to pay more.

This is the second time a make the mistake to switch from Verizon to o t-mobile. I completely forgot how impossible it is to cancel service. Well I switched back to tmobile because I would save money but unfortunately their network was not as good as Verizon I was not getting service if I was outside of the city. So I told them this was the only reason I by I was switching back to Verizon. Well the list just became longer as Verizon Customer Service compared to tmobile A++ and when they disconnect the line they call you back. I wish I could say the same for TMobile. 
September 2021 I switch back to Verizon. October I call to cancel because I switched back to Verizon. The guy tells me he canceled my monthly payments. I told him and explained I would any the entire plan canceled so watch, phone and tablet. Ok he said he did. December 15  I call back because I have a regular monthly bill for $39 I speak to Kelly Anderson she said it was suspended but she canceled it and to wait for the final bill. I vetoed watch, tablet and phone. Skipping to Bill I get end of January for $128.07 I call 1-800-937-8997 mad because it includes previous and current balance. I cannot speak to a service rep because my account is suspended due to non pay. So now I got to the store speak with Xavier who  helps me calls the service dept we speak to Thessaloniki Aliado 2/1 at 1:14 she cancels the account opens a ticket so to request to have the account canceled retroactive back to October and to call the in 2-3 days as the system should be updated with final bill. Today 2/3/22 I receive a call from tmobile collection department At 10:23 Am calling me to pay $128.17. This guy said it shows canaceled as of 2/1 but I need to now pay $128.17. I am x plainer the above transfers me to MES idk if that is really his name and this guy resolved nothing. He asked me to pay $39.89 and he opened a ticket to have the account canceled back to October. I asked what happen to other ticket he said it was removed today. So nothing was resolved I asked him two different times to speak to a manager because it just felt like he was lost in figuring things out and making things up without knowing 100% so he finally transferred me or put me on hold well call disconnected and no one called me back and I have been writing this review since they disconnected me. I told the rep that once the coverage expanded to other areas I planned to come back. But based on this horrible service I will never come back and rather pay more for more coverage and quality service through Verizon. I have no idea if they will resolve the bill but the store will know my face until it does. Also when I was at the store there as another person in their for the same thing.

I recently went into a new store at Battle Ridge Pavilion, Powder Springs Rd, Marietta, GA 30064.  I am not a customer but just needed help getting a new case on my iphone.  There were 3 employees there and each one greeted me with a smile and assistance!  I was impressed. Good job, guys!!

The worse customer service I ever had.  I got a new iPhone 12, after calling customer service and hearing it worked, I got off.  Not only did it not work, now my old iPhone is not working either.  I just called and asked if they would at least activate my old iPhone - I hear o yes not a problem.  2 hours into being on the phone with these incompetent reps, my iPhone is still not working.  Regretfully I had a huge customer service call I missed.  Would not recommend this company.

I agree with the negative reviews here.  The customer 
service who answers the phone don't know how to solve issues,   they send bill repetitively even though I cancel  the service,  etc....  
There's nothing positive  I could tell.

T-Mobile/Sprint are criminals. I bought a phone from T-Mobile and the they had a promo get a free watch when you open a new line. I was a Sprint customer and my bill was 35 dollars a month. So I added the line for 10 a month+ 35 monthly bill, + insurance on the line. My first bill was 100 and the second was 100. I called customer service to inquire why my bill was so high. They told me no you had to get two additional lines, purchase 1 watch and get the other one free. First, I do lease devices and I would have just paid for the watch upfront just like I paid for the phone. Long story short, I canceled the service. So today I go to Verizon. When I talked to sales rep he checked to make sure my phone was unlocked and it was. Then I called T-Mobile to get my account number. As soon as I got the account number, They put a lock on my phone and watch. Almost 1000 dollars of equipment that I own. I told this is illegal and coercive monster tactics to either continue my service with them or loose my devices. Run and tell everyone do not use TMobile/Sprint.

Upon attempting to do an upgrade (being a sprint customer) which is now t-mobile IT LITERALLY HAS BEEN THE WORST CONSUMER EXPERIENCE OF MY LIFE! First of all you speak to someone in the Philippines or anywhere else. You're lucky to speak with someone in the U.S. Next the customer service is horrible they transfer you or lie or both. I can go on but what's the point "They Suck" Do yourself a favor and go somewhere else!

I was going to switch to T-Mobile from Consumer Cellular but changed my mind just before I inserted my sim chip. It’s been a NIGHTMARE trying to delete my account between their phone operators (putting me on hold and NEVER RETURNING) OR SENDING ME TO THEIR LOCAL STORE and getting ZERO HELP. Then they have been sending me bills (3 months worth now). I went through FairShake to resolve this. I then had a T-Mobile employee call me and he said he would kill my bill and account which NEVER HAPPENED BECAUSE I JUST RECEIVED MY THIRD BILL. NOW they still want me to pay $20.00 and no mention of killing my account. 

It is obvious that T-Mobile has ZERO CLASS and I hold CEO MIKE SIEVERT responsible for allowing his employees to act in this manner. I am so glad I didn’t switch to this CLASSLESS COMPANY. IF ANYONE IS THINKING ABOUT SWITCHING TO T-Mobile, do yourself a big favor and DON’T!!!!!

edits.text: I have been a T-Mobile client for over 20 years.  After the debackle of 2020 and all of these companies forcing whites to believe we are racist, or to believe we are privileged solely due to the color of our skin is NOT ACCEPTABLE from a company who provides CELL PHONE SERVICE.  Stick to what you know.  I will be reviewing other cell phone companies who DO NOT use T-Mobile towers for service to switch to.  I hope you do the same thing - our VOICE and our VOTE are all that we have.

text: If I could give zero stars I would. I joined T mobile in June and it had been the biggest nightmare ever. Every single month it is problem after problem. When I signed on I was told I would get a free phone. Get my first bill and it is over $200 and they charged me for my phone. Called and then they tell me that because the promotion I signed up under wasn't going anymore that I couldn't get it, even though it was active when I signed up. Everyone there always, and I mean always, has a different story and the one told to you before was a lie. I am disabled and don't get much money to live on and I spoke to them about taking the money for my bill just 2 days later. I was told that was no problem and they had fixed it. Wake up on the original payment day and have a message they took the money even after telling me they wouldn't. I am now in the hole and had to even miss a surgical appointment because they took out the money when they said they wouldn't. And now they just want to credit my bill for next month. They put me in a hole I am having a hard time getting out of. Run far away from T mobile at all costs because all they are are thieves and don't care about their customers. Absolute nightmare

I was with Sprint for years and they were bought out by T Mobile. Back in July, they had a promotion going on if you migrated your account with T Mobile, you basically got a free phone when turning in your old phone. I asked the associate at the South Tampa Gandy store if my bill would go up, and he informed me it would by about $10-$15 a month. Fast forward to a few months later and my bill went up around $40 a month. I called TMobile 1800 # and after going back and forth for an hour, the customer service rep asked if he could call me back the next night once he spoke to his manager around the same time. This was around 7pm my time. The next night I don't get a call, but I wake up to a voice mail from the customer service rep, who called me at 1:30am and told me there was nothing he could do for me. Yes, I said 1:30 am . I went back to the store and the associate who helped me was no longer there, but another gentleman was. He looked at my bill and said that in order to give me the deal I was promised, he couldn't make my monthly bill the promised amount I was told, but he would take $80 a month off for the next 6 months and that would basically even things out. I agreed to that. After my bill being $80 less that month, the following  bill was back to the $123 it had been. Not the $43 the gentleman told me it would be for 6 months. I went back to the store again, but this time the store was closed down. Finally having enough of their shadiness, I paid the iPhone off, so I could have the phone unlocked and went with a different carrier. if you do business with TMobile, you are getting what you ask for.

T Mobile is a rip off people. Do not go to t mobile and buy any equipment or you will regret it big time. If you do have to buy something at t Mobile be sure you do not go to a 3rd party store that says they are T mobile. Read below what has happen to me and a lot of other customers I am sure. 
I went to a 3rd party T Mobile store Christmas Eve. I went there that day and signed up for 2 phone lines and bought 2 Apple 13 pro max phones. That was the last day to get free Airpods if I signed up for new service. I placed the order for the service, the phones and I even bought a apple watch there that day. I also ordered the Airpods. The sales person that waited on me was named Ramon. The store is located at the Eastgate Dr store in Cincinnati Ohio on Eastgate drive, not the Eastage Mall store. The salesman even got the airpods out of the case and was going to let me take them at first but then he said the Airpods would be shipped with the Iphones so I said OK. I GUESS I SHOULD HAVE SAID NO AND TOOK THEM WITH ME. The Iphones came in the mail a week later but there was no Airpods. I called the salesman to ask him why I did not get the airpods. The person at that 3rd party store said my Salesman Ramon was not there that day but Airpods have been being backordered because they ran out. So I waited another week but I did not get them so I tried to call Ramon again. This time the person answering the phone said Ramon my salesman did not work there anymore. I can only assume he got fired because he does not do his job.. He sure did not do his job with me.. I told him what happen to me and he said I would have to talk to the store manager (Amara) but she was not there then. So I called back when she was there and she said I could come in and see what can be done. I went there and she said there is nothing she can do since Ramon my salesman did not put the Airpods on my order form with my Iphone order. Keep in mind I have never bought a service or any equipment from T Mobile before in my life. I thought I could trust what I was told by the salesman and what we said to each other. I thought Ramon placed the order with my Phones since I told him to and he told me he did. I even asked him for a paper copy of what I signed on his Ipad and he said his printer was not working but I could get a copy from my T Mobile account page. I called T Mobile main office also and they said my Salesman Ramon had never placed the order for my Airpods. Now the T Mobile 3rd party store says they cannot help me since their salesman Ramon did not put the Free Airpods on my order with the phones. I am ready to cancel my service with T Mobile and just pay the full amount of my phones off and go to another cell service company now, since I see what kind of help I get from T Mobile. I will regret and remember what they did to me each time I pay my monthly bill for the next 30 months. 
WARNING: NEVER BUY ANYTHING FROM T MOBILE BUT IF YOU DO PLEASE DO NOT GO TO A 3RD PARTY T MOBILE STORE. I WILL BE WARNING EVERYONE ABOUT WHAT HAPPEN TO ME AT T MOBILE AND I WILL FOR SURE TELL EVERYONE IN THE CINCINNATI OHIO AREA TO NEVER GO TO THE T MOBILE 3RD PARTY STORE IN THE EASTGATE AREA ON EASTGATE DR. I AM GOING TO TRY MY BEST TO MAKE SURE THIS 3RD PARTY STORE LOSES A LOT MORE THAN THE COST OF THE AIRPODS THEY RIPPED ME OFF OF. MAYBE T MOBILE WILL PULL THEIR LICENSE’S WITH THEM ALSO. PLEASE EVERYONE SHARE THIS WITH EVERYONE ALL OVER THE WORLD SO OTHERS KNOW WHAT TYPE OF COMPANY T MOBILE IS. MAYBE WE CAN MAKE T MOBILE A BETTER COMPANY THAT TAKES CARE OF THEIR CUSTOMERS BETTER THAN THEY DID ME. I am retired and I am home ALL DAY so I will be on the internet most of the day spreading the word about what T Mobile and that 3rd party store did to me. Post your problems you had with T Mobile also. I told Amara I was going to tell everyone on social media I could about what happen to me and it would cost T Mobile more than what those Airpods would have cost them but Amara replied back “that is OK because I will still have my job”

They billed and charged me twice with two different amounts for final bill and would not correct it. in-addition the advertisement about the 5G Wireless internet is a hype, it didn't work as expected and they sold me a booster they don't have support for and gave me the run around when trying to work with them to fix the issue. will never come back to them and will always give bad reviews any chance I get throughout social media and my inner circle contacts as long as they are not willing to fix the issue, I am speaking from experience and have been with them for 14 years and yet they would not refund any amount or be willing to solve the issue.

My husband is charged with purchasing 2 IPhones and accessories from another state. Someone stole his identity to make these purchases, and the deposits were made to our credit card. I contacted the credit card company and they immediately recognized the charges as fraudulent and reversed them, cancelled the account, and issued new cards. Angel at T-Mobile said they had already flagged the purchase as suspicious, and by contacting them I confirmed it. The fraud dept would take care of it, but I could consider getting a police report, though it wasn't necessary.  Then, the first collection letter comes. I call again the talk to Brittany, who claims they can't close the issue because we didn't file a police report. Fast forward, we are now on our third collection agency, the state police officer that we filed the complaint with said that under no circumstances she we pay any part of this bill. An attorney general's report was filed, which T-Mobile disputed, claiming they completed their own investigation and determined that someone using my husband's identity purchased 2 IPhones and accessories. They will wave the late fees and additional charges if we give them back. I have spent untold hours trying to fix their mistake. Were the phones ever activated, were they purchased in person, were they pinged? T-Mobile's investigation was a joke, and I will continue to fight. We cannot return what we never had. We have never owned an IPhone (too expensive) and have an excellent credit rating that I would not risk for cell phones.

I was a Sprint customer and since T Mobile has taken over service has been horrible as is customer service.  I damaged the screen to my phone and went to a local T Mobile store (was a Sprint store) and they informed me that only two T Mobile stores in the metro area now did repairs.  At the repair store they told me I couldn't be helped till I went online to make an appointment.  Making an appointment doesn't happen at the T Mobile site though and when you search online to make an appointment the actual site doesn't come up.  Went back in the store to get help setting an appointment but because the screen of my phone didn't work it filled information in wrong.  Went back to the repair store, explained what happened and they tried to help but couldn't actually look at the phone because policy made it so I HAD to have the appointment.  They called the third party that handled this and were informed they couldn't do anything and I had to call.  I was also informed that the insurance I paid for with Sprint was not being honored by T Mobile so instead of a $30 repair I had to pay $100 dollars for a new phone.  Called T Mobile but couldn't remember the pin for our account so they said they would call me back in an hour after I had found it.  That was several days ago.  I finally got the claim filed online for a new phone only after calling a local store to get help.  Still had to pay the $100 for the new phone however.  As soon as possible my family and I are jumping ship off of T Mobile.

T-mobile is having lots of issues with their network. But, that can be fixed. What T-mobile is failing to fix is their very poor customer service. They lie to get your business and when they are caught, they do not try to fix the issue and instead start telling you “rest assured, we will give you best customer service.” It’s funny that they wasted over 6 hours of my time in their online chatting over the course of 4 days and still couldn’t fix the billing issue. It was escalated to 4 different people and 4 supervisors. They all lied for 4 days. I am hoping that somebody from T-mobile will read these comments and try to fix things before their multiple loyal customers goes to their competitors. Very very poor customer service on their chat function. I don’t think they are American employees. Takes over 5 minutes and sometimes even takes 15 plus minutes to answer simple questions. It’s better not to have chat function because it’s not solving issues but making customers angry with their very poor skills to solve issues in hand.

UPDATE!!!!!
T MOBILE MADE IT RIGHT AGAIN!!!!
I have been with TMobile for almost 18 years and things were great until now. A representative offered me an additional line on my plan for $0 per month so I purchased an additional phone and added it on only to find that they are now charging me $30 per month for that line. Many calls and escalated up to managers and they are telling me their rep was new and wrong to offer that deal and they refuse to fix it. The old bait and switch. Looks like it’s time to switch.
Customer service is pathetic.  English language skills poor.  Wait times extensive.  Not clear what they are saying.  Frustrating.

T-Mobile happens to be the worst trustworthy mobile provider.  They charged for a phone that was returned on our final bill.  In addition, they pro-rated our billing cycle for 17 days, and then charged a single day for the balance of a full month charge.  Apparently, they do NOT pro-rate billing if you change providers mid cycle.

Not to mention, the reason we left T-Mobile was because the service was horrible.  I was told we would have full signal at both my office and at home.  Neither was true.  T-Mobile had to send me Cell Spots for both locations that relied on my internet service.  If internet was down, I had no reliable cell phone service.

The customer services are utterly rode and useless. In a case that the representatives does not know the answer to your question, they will start repeating the useless information. T-Mobile teach your representatives to think and at least try to find a solution for your customer's issue instead of repeating like a parrot.
They are just utterly useless. Nothing works. Customer support is terrible,

I was switching over from Metro after being with them for 3 years only because I was sent multiple emails telling me to switch over to T-Mobile because I've been with Metro paid my bill every month I would not be charged a down payment and also no credit check well I ordered my phone on November 21st and it was sad to be delivered on the December the 15th, needless to say I have not received my phone! I have spoken with customer service, order status, chat and every possible way that I can get in touch with someone that would help me but I was lied to you each time was told that my order was going to be updated and I was receiving email. 
I never received the email, my order status was not updated so I contacted them once again and they said that they have my phone in stock but I would have to process my order through T-Mobile run a credit check put a  down payment. Completely deceptive, I asked you to speak with management but she said that management has the same access to all of the information that she has, needless to say I am furious, mostly because I was deceived. If this is the way that they start doing their business then I'm sure that there were only be issues in the future!!
 I'm staying with my current phone company which I haven't had any issues with. I'm so glad that I did not transfer over to T-Mobile / Sprint!

I called tmobile today to repair a phone I bought from Bestbuy. I spoke with a Rep named Leslie today 1/23/22. She was very helpful & courteous she even stayed on the phone while I spoke to a repair technician . She went above & beyond to make sure that I was going to get a repair ticket before exiting off of the call. I'm very grateful for her superb customer service (some might say it's her job)because I know she didn't have to do it! Thank you Leslie & to all the Leslie's that really love assisting customers & resolving issues!

I had a very bad experience with the T-mobile store at Cochran in Simi Valley.The only person who really helped was Alan. It all started when I tried calling for an appointment so that I transfer my service from AT&T. The website for the store indicated  they open at 10:00 am. I started calling at 10:00 and rang for an hour but no one answered. Finally I decided to try the t-mobile chat. I was told that someone would help me right away but I waited for more than 15 minute before someone answered at which point I got a completely lazy inadequate person that kept asking me questions which I responded. It sounded like I was chatting with a child, I would probably described that person as absolutely not a smart person. I would  respond but the person will take forever to respond to me and will not acknowledge my answers. Finally after 30 minutes of getting nowhere I decided to hang up and the person had the audacity’s to say “thank you for chatting with me”. Who does t-mobile higher for those jobs??? At that point I decided to just go to the store. I spoke with Bryan who seemed a nice guy and supposedly he took care of the transfer, but that was not the case. He also told me that hours had changed because of COVID but this is now almost 4 years since it started shouldn’t they have updated the website?? Bryan made a mistake and neglected to let me know that in addition to the pass code from AT&T I also needed a transfer pin. So the transfer he was supposed never happen. At the 3rd visit to the store trying to resolve the problem I finally spoke with Alan who at my 4th visit to the store was able to resolve the problem. I spoke to 2 managers who kept bringing up excuses for what had happened and not acknowledging that the matter had not been handled properly and I have been grossly inconvenienced with loss of my time. I told them that Bryan had made a mistake and they should acknowledge that and see that as as learning experience. The African American manager finally acknowledged it and apologized. The Latino manager didn’t. By the way I am Latina myself and my grandmother is half black so no racism here. On top of this while Alan was diligently trying to help me with the transfer, a short Latino sales person whose name unfortunately I didn’t get, approached me at the tall table where I was standing next to Alan  asked me to move to another table because he “had a client”. Alan was making phone calls to AT&T from a phone connected to the table where I was.  I had been standing for a while and needed to sit down. I have Parkinson’s and couldn’t stand any longer so I told the sales person “no” and I pointed out to the fact that Alan was busy there. The sales person made a face and walked away. I couldn’t believe how rude he was towards me and towards his own colleague. I spoke with the 2 managers  again and explained to them what had just transpired. I told them that I have Parkinson’s and needed to sit. The African American manager said to me”that’s why the guy suggested I sit at the table.” I had to tell him that I never disclosed to the salesman or Alan that I have Parkinson’s.  What do you think about that? Both managers kept coming up with excuses until finally the African American manager said that he will talk to the salesperson. I have never been treated like that. This was my 4th visit to resolve the transfer of service and I was hoping to buy an iPhone 13. I don’t know if I will return to this store. If I do, it will only be to see Alan and to give that salesperson a piece of my mind for being so rude. All in all it has been a nightmare dealing with these people and their service from the very beginning.  Ah an I will note that on the times I was there I saw 2 other people that were not very happy themselves. Very dissatisfied and disappointed  with the quality of service I received there other than Alan’s help. Yes, Bryan was nice but clearly he wasn’t knowledgeable of what was needed to properly transfer the service.  The Latino manager should also be retrained as well. On top of it, before I left he said to me “we heard you”. I don’t think so and that’s why I wrote this long and detailed review.

The free promotional items you get are the trap so that you can never leave Tmobile. I have two sub par tablets useless, that you can't ever leave the airtime because you never get them paid off. They delete records of telephone conversations with you then claim the conversations never took place.  If you call to try to speak with a supervisor, they simply leave you on hold until you finally hang up.

I would give zero stars if possible. Been a long time customer of Sprint and they were bought by t mobile. I bought my daughter an iPhone 13 for Christmas and 2 days later I saw a commercial for a buy one get one free iPhone 13 deal for all existing customers. I called customer service and they tried to tell me it was Only for T-Mobile customers but that is not what the commercial says. They wouldn’t give the other iPhone. They wouldn’t transfer me to a manager and they are false advertising.

I've been with t-mobile for many years.  Generally speaking,  the service has been pretty good.  However, today (20January2022) I've had the most awful experience with them. I do not recommend phone service because it has a double edge signal two ridges or a type of diffraction weakening between me and the cell towers and the download speeds are very weak like for example 0.62Mbps and the upload speed could be very low too for example of 0.98Mbps if you try to use this service anywhere you get you get that poor reception on your cell phone signal strength meter! ⚠️ Warning  don't buy t-mobile phone!⚠️

STAY AWAY!!!
We had a family plan with Sprint before the 
T-Mobile merger. The customer service was already horrible, but everything got worse after the merger.
Our phones no longer had service, we could not text and calls dropped off constantly.
When we called, they said “have you swapped out your SIM card?”.
No, we didn’t know there was a SIM card swap.
Once we did get new SIM cards, and spent 3 hours on each phone trying to get the new cards to function, the service got even worse.
I can stand in front of the Sprint/T mobile store in Cypress Tx, and get ZERO signal.
Unfortunately we have had to endure 2 years of s@&? service until getting to an affordable exit strategy.
If they offer free phones, free watches and free service for 10 years, TURN AND WALK AWAY , or you will regret it for years.

So I have never been nor will I ever be an account holder with this extremely horrible company. My identity was used over the phone to purchase three devices for a grand total of $5000.00 helped by a Ma Beniza (name given to me by the oy helpful customer rep so far). No information was confirmed by this individual to confirm that this person was who they said they were. So when I stated to get the statements for these devices, went right to a t-mobile store, they couldn't help me at all  and to call cs and talk to a fraud agent ( which by the way they don't have). The individual I spoke with at first made me feel like I was just trying to get out of paying for these devices (now I'm the criminal trying to fix a problem) then for some reason his attitude changed and he supposedly filled out a form (#58583901) and I was told someone would call me in 2 to 4 business days (really for fraud, so much for caring thanks) to this day (over a month later) no has called me. So now waiting on hold (2 hours and counting) to attempt to fix this issue again. I have even received a bill for these devices, So that fraud form was never accomplished or t-mobile just doesn't give a damn about consumers. Thank God I'm with Verizon for over 20 years, never had an issue with them, security on personal information is tight,  and have never been on hold for over 5 minutes when I do need to talk to cs agent.

I bought a new phone for my sister because she had misplaced hers while spending 4 weeks in the hospital due to kidney failure.  She is in a nursing home and needed a phone - she is 76.  I bought the phone, then was told to activate it, she would need to call customer service and add me as a user to her account.  We spent an hour on a conference call with t-mobil customer service on 1/18/22 and they assured us I was added.  I got to the store to have them activate the phone only to be told I wasn't a user.  So, she had to call again from the nursing home main number and be on hold for nearly an hour before they finally got me added - again!  T-Mobil needs to have something in place where a family member can help a relative in a nursing home without spending THREE DAYS trying to get them a phone activated with their current number.  She gave the store employee all her information while on the phone and he still refused, claiming he couldn't verify who she was even though she gave them the account number, passcode, etc.  This is appalling!  Trying to help an elderly relative shouldn't be so hard!

Just dropped into the Alhambra store to get a credit card updated on file and the customer service by the male service member was appalling. I think it was Jose. He made no attempt to help as i have a foreign account and the account wouldnt accept the zip code. He told me that i needed to call customer service and when i told him i was told by CS to come to a local store he just shrugged his shoulders and gave me the big $%@# off attitude. Funny thing is with three staff in the store and no customers he could jave helped problem solve the situation but was arrogant and rude with his reponses. Again another reponse that sits outside his box and he was so unhelpful when i was just trying to pay my account. I will be reporting him elsewhere.
I have been with T-Mobile 5+ years and ever since they merged with Sprint they are garbage they are in too deep and bit off more than they can chew. Horrible customer service

Only gave 1 star because they don’t have an option to give less than one star. There is no “black hole” option, seriously, that’s how much I despise this company. I’ve been a loyal T-Mobile US customer for a decade. Never missed a payment. Have paid a super premium for only one line, between $115 & $125. Time and time again I’ve been misled or lied to by a very insidious “team of experts”(Experts in BS maybe…) The Insincerity is palpable, tangible in fact. You can feel it, your instincts tell you you’re being lied to… But they’re very good at deceiving you so against your better judgment… For goodness sake, if your going to lie, believe it yourself, be creative, don’t tell a lie that people will realize is a lie ten minutes after you tell the lie. Have integrity to that lie, otherwise it’s just plain insulting. It’s a horrible feeling when you realize your the victim of lies. I’ve had to constantly, throughout the relationship, fix mistakes pertaining to billing, promised benefits, the list is to long. As a matter of fact, it’s not worth the time to even write this review. Plain and simple the BOTTOM LINE. Pricing is never as promised-there’s always something hidden from you or you simply were never enrolled in a promised promotion or plan. Never believe what you’re being told by this company get it in writing and ask for clarification if they won’t provide it don’t do it. I only gave one star because otherwise I wouldn’t be allowed to write a review. They should have a sub star rating system. Something that is less than a star or opposite of a star like say a black hole. That’s what I give T-Mobile 4 black holes. A customer service team that is taught and trained to LIE! Just finally got fed up with the BS. 
I have over 10K in T-Mobile US stock I purchased in 2007 for Approximately $40.10 or $41.20, about 243 shares, now worth $106.20 (Thank you John Legere) I’m curious, I wonder if I liquidated today how many options I could purchase of Verizon? I think I’m about to find out? So $25,748 minus closing fees. Verizon is aprox. $50 per share, that’s roughly 500 shares and Verizon pays dividends. Yes I think I’ll liquidate my T-Mobile shares, purchase Verizon and switch my service to Verizon as well. All thanks to the wonderful customer service interaction I had today, January 18, 2022. Thanks T-Mobile for a decade of lies, unkept promises, promotions that never materialized, barely reliable service, untrustworthy customer service, feeling undervalued and unappreciated as a customer. I hope people read this review and consider my experience before committing to T-Mobile. Perhaps if they continue to not retain customers they will change. However it is my opinion that they’ll become another AT&T. No longer is T-Mobile the “un-carrier” but rather the un-Caring, un-appreciative, un- responsive Carrier and that’s really Un- fortunate for existing customers. 
Unapologetically,
J. Malone

The worst phone company I ever have to deal with. After having constant billing problems, I gave up with this company and now I been trying to cancel my account with them over and over again, but they keep me on hold for 3h and hang up on me. Terrible phone customer service and terrible store service, they don't care about you. DON'T GET AN ACCOUNT WITH THEM!!!
Tmobil sucks. I can’t believe anyone still uses them.

After 11 different people I spoke to through Sprint and T-Mobile I finally got someone that I could understand and also that he knew what he was talking about. His name was Tony and he saved the day. He was very polite he explained everything thoroughly and I really appreciate him and the time he took for my daughters phone.

We signed up for T Mobile in September 2021 and have been happy with the service, pricing, and coverage since then, but the billing has been an absolute nightmare. It is now January 2022 and the monthly billing is still not correct. I'm on the phone with them right now about the January bill. This is the seventh or eighth time, I think, but it could be more. I've called so many times I've lost track. We were supposed to get two free phones as part of the promotion available when we signed up. They have consistently charged us for one of the two phones each month as well as other random charges that varied month to month. At this point I have a hard time recommending T-Mobile due to these billing problems.

I have been a Sprint customer for almost 30 years and have never had my service shut off. Since they merged with TMobile I have been with them 1 month and have been in the store or on the phone every week. My first payment went to my Sprint account so they shut my whole phone off. They gave me a watch that was supposed to be free when I got my bill the watch is $15 a month and has a whole separate phone line and number. When I tried to take it back the store I got it from wouldn't take it because it was past 14 days and the customer service line said I can't mail it in because I didn't get it on line. So I'm stuck with a watch I don't use or need and was lied to about it from the beginning. If I could give them 0 stars I would. I would not recommend them to anyone and my intent is to get as far away from them as I can as soon as I can. Shame on you for treating Veterans and loyal Sprint customers the way you do. Smh

I tried to cancel within the 20 day window and have 0 balance as they advertise. So far, I called 5 times, spent many hours on hold, and 3 out of 5 times I called the customer service rep hung up on me. The other 2 times, the rep told me they'd send a shipping label, but it never came. Every time I call, I keep getting bounced around or disconnected without resolution.

Don’t believe the ads you see for T-Mobile… I went into the store they had three service representatives and it took me 45 minutes for I get one to wait on me… Only to be told the plan that they advertised on TV was only available to certain areas as they didn’t want to overload the system… But they don’t tell you that it’s only offered in a very small area the home Internet $50 unlimited… What a waste of my time! Beware of T-Mobile advertising blitz they are very misleading

I wish I had read some of these reviews before going  to T Mobile.    I was told in the store by the clerk that my plan would cost a certain amount each month and verified that later with the store manager.   When I received my bill it was double the amount they quoted me.    I spent numerous hours on the phone and back to the store only to be told that the offer that store offered me in Dec 2021 was only for existing customers effective  9/2021  Three months earlier I tried to get this corrected but of course it toolk over two weeks for anyone to tell me the problem.   Just enough time to void the 14 day time frame to cancel with no penalties.   The store manager admitted that they told me the incorrect plan  and said she was sorry but the only thing she could  do is the lower my account  to the essential plan which is a crappy plan.   I was tricked into my plan and now I am stuck  for  30 months unless I want to pay full amount for 4 phones.     REAL RIPOFF company   Sad that they have to trick people into signing with them.    If you go with this company be sure to get EVERYTHING they tell you in wriiting.

T-Mobile is the worst of the phone companies. They get you in the door and I later found out that I didn’t get the plan they promised. I’ve tried reaching out to their customer service many times with no resolution.

Nothing but trouble. They must train their people, from the "store" people to the customer service "team"(which English is not their first language) how to be shady and pull cards from their sleeves. The plan we wanted was not offered in the "store" nor were we offered anything even close. One nightmare after another. DO NOT GO WITH T MOBILE

WORST PHONE COMPANY EVER.

They treat customers absolutely awfully.
They disconnect your line and dont even try to fix it.
Horrible internet speed.
They got hacked last year with personal info about the customers.
EVERYTHING IS ABSOLUTELY HORRIBLE ABOUT THIS COMPANY.

Terrible, terrible company!!! Every customer service person tells you a different thing, and they will even deny what is written on their own website! They straight up lied to us about the trade in deal we were getting for our phones, and then are ridiculously difficult to deal with about returning a phone. We've wasted hours and hours and hours of our lives with these horrible customer service people--and we've only been with them a month! I will switch companies ASAP and will never, ever, give them business again. Worst company I have ever dealt with. No idea how they stay in business. So incompetent and frustrating.

Each call with them on hold 3 hrs till your phone disconnect then a fraud breakthrough happens we were unlucky and two watches got bought with our info we tried contacting them they wouldn't own up to the responsibility now we are stuck paying 2 lines and watches they wont even give us free watches I'm quiting

Very, very, very bad. Went in to the store to take advantage of an advertised deal on a new phone. The first phone my husband got worked only intermittently & kept giving the message that there was no sim in the phone (there was). Went to the store & got a new sim & the same thing happened. Went to the store & they ordered a new phone. The "new" phone was a refurbished phone (not what we paid for) & it also claims either there is no sim or the sim is invalid. Got a replacement phone from customer service. It arrived today. It is also refurbished (again, not we we paid for). We're done with T-Mobile. I was a Sprint customer for 22 years. T-Mobile sucks

They suck, one agent will tell you one thing and another agent will tell you something different. No one will honor what the previous agent tells you. Of course, their all sorry but that doesn't do me any good. All I wanted was for them to honor what I was told. All the agents seem to just say what they want. I wouldn't recommend them.

Unbelievable really! First time I needed t mobile installed, it took me 3 months and at least 25 calls to customer service! Now I moved houses and unfortunately I needed to continue with them due to the one year contract and AGAIN same problem! I am literally explaining them what they have to do because it happened to me already and NOTHING! Still paying for not having internet! STAY AWAY FROM THIS COMPANY IF YOU HAVE NOT SIGNED A CONTRACT YET.
Stay away from T Mobile!!! The customer representatives are a bunch of liars. Everytime that I called I was lied to.

My daughter brought a new phone online since it wasn't available in the store.. She mailed back her old phone this Oct 2022, with the packaging and label sent from Tmobile.

On 12/26/2021 I saw my bill was $1800. I contacted Tmobile and they agreed to do a search. A week later I received. VM from a Supervisor from Tmobile saying they couldn't find the phone.

I contacted UPS and spoke to Ms. North, who confirm the phone did arrive at Ft Worth, Tx at their warehouse. In also received the tracking confirmation.  

The phone not worth $1800 used. I can't get help from anyone at Tmobile and I can't wait to cancel my plan.

If you google TMobile, Ft Worth Tx. warehouse she will see alot of google reviews that people are be asking to pay for their phone because its not showing in the warehouse.

BEWARE DO NOT SHIP YOUR PHONE!!!!
Used to be great customer service. Now that they've merged with Sprint its terrible foreign representatives who try to fumble through to help you instead of the local people they used to have.

I’ve been a T-Mobile customer for almost a year.  I bought Apple Air Pods online from them and immediately discovered I purchased the wrong ones.  My bad. But, I contacted them immediately and tried to stop delivery.  They said no but that I should decline delivery from UPS.  I did that, never took possession of the Air Pods. Subsequently, I was told by various reps that once the warehouse receives the return I could expect a refund in “3” “5” and “7” days.  One rep told me 10 days.  Reps are not allowed to call the warehouse for status they are only allowed to send them emails.  It’s been 35 days and I have not received an updated status nor my refund.  T-Mobile is highly efficient in getting payment but a disaster in making it right for the customer.  Don’t buy anything online from T-Mobile.
All they care about is their money! Greedy ass company...don't get covid and laid up for months...they don't care!

Tmobile is the worst cell phone service in the country.  Their customer service ridiculous.   Ot always takes at least 2 hours to get a call back.  Each so called expert gives different information.   I had 2 phones on my account and was told the iPhone was blocked to my grandson steal it and go to another carrier.   I was told 3 times the block was there and fond out now that wasn't true.  Now I'm stuck paying for a phone.   And if you report it stolen I have to pay 250.00 deductible.    DONT GO TO TMOBILE@!!!!
I lost service and I've been trying to chat with customer service for 3 hours now. No response,  nothing! Terrible service
